Learn to profile and optimize agentic AI on Windows AI PCs, correlating CPU, GPU, and NPU telemetry to cut latency and power with OpenVINO and Windows ML.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Profile and Optimize Agentic AI on WindowsThis article is a practical guide to profiling and optimizing agentic AI that runs locally on a Windows AI PC. I will walk through where a local agent actually spends its time and power across the CPU, GPU, and NPU, how to capture hardware and software telemetry on a single correlated timeline, and how to act on what you find.
By the end, you will know the tools involved (Intel Unified Telemetry, the Instrumentation and Tracing Technology API, OpenVINO and OpenVINO GenAI, and Windows ML with its CLI), and you will have a repeatable method: profile the workflow, analyze where the time goes, then optimize by moving work to the right engine and tuning each tool. The goal is simple: turn “my local agent feels slow” into a measurable problem you can fix.
Reference here.
Why local iteration speed is the metric that matters
Powerful models that used to require a data center now run on thin-and-light laptops. When inference moves on-device, the thing you are optimizing changes. You are no longer paying per cloud token. You are paying in turns: how many prompt-and-response cycles you can…
